topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #17 Tier 2 (RB7, TEN landing), RSP #14 DoT 84.1, ADP 25.83 — all three boards converge near our 2.04 slot, and RB is our thinnest skill spot at 4 deep. The Tennessee opportunity plus blue-chip recruit pedigree makes him the cleanest value at 2.04 if he slides past picks 1.13-1.17.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #13 Tier 2 (RB5, KC), RSP #8 DoT 84.6 'Rotational Starter' — RSP loves him more than market does, and KC backfield path is real. With my 25% RSP weight he's a viable 2.04 if Singleton's gone; medium-affinity GM doesn't reach for him over Consensus, but he sits at the top of Tier 2 RB.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #21 Tier 3 (TE2, PHI), RSP #11 DoT 84.4, ADP 21.25 — Philly landing with a converted-QB athletic profile. At 2.11 (~pick 27) he's the RSP/ADP sweet spot, and even with 4 TEs rostered he's a clear long-term TE1 upgrade over depth pieces.

topTargets.name: Demond Claiborne
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #26 Tier 4 (RB8, MIN), RSP #9 DoT 84.5 — RSP has him 17 spots higher than Consensus does. Classic medium-affinity buy at 2.11: Vikings backfield is unsettled, RSP comp is Shady McCoy, and RB need lets me weight the 25% RSP signal hard here.

topTargets.name: Kaytron Allen
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #14 Tier 2 (RB6, WAS) — pure Consensus play if he drops. WAS workload path is the cleanest in this RB tier. RSP doesn't list him in top 30, so my medium weighting still backs the pick because Consensus is 50% of my board and RB need is real.

capPosture: Tight at $4.3M space with 24 active and 3 contracts expiring; rookies likely go taxi (50% hit) to stay under the $45M cap, especially the 2.04 pick.

wildcard.name: Antonio Williams
wildcard.reasoning: Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WR14, WAS), RSP #18 DoT 83.9 — RSP comps him to Jayden Reed and tags him a 'potential bargain.' At 2.11 if RB run thins the board, the medium-affinity GM peeks at the RSP-favored WR despite being WR-heavy already.

summary: With no first-rounder and a WR-stacked, RB-thin roster, Bring The Pain is hunting RB depth at 2.04 and best-available with RSP lean at 2.11. Cap squeeze ($4.3M) forces both picks onto the taxi squad at 50% salary. Medium RSP affinity means I anchor on Consensus Tier 2 RBs (Singleton, Johnson, Allen) at 2.04, then let RSP's voice break ties at 2.11 between Stowers, Claiborne, and Williams.
